findings.title: Majority of binaries lack stack canaries
findings.description: 119/120 binaries have no stack canary. Stack buffer overflow exploitation is easier.

findings.title: Majority of binaries lack PIE
findings.description: 104/120 binaries are not position-independent. ASLR bypass is easier with known base addresses.

findings.title: Binaries without NX (executable stack)
findings.description: 120 binaries have executable stack. Stack-based shellcode execution is possible.

findings.title: Critical unsafe C functions used across firmware
findings.description: Dangerous functions found: gets(1 binaries), strcpy(30 binaries), sprintf(29 binaries), strcat(11 binaries), scanf(1 binaries). These functions do not perform bounds checking and are common sources of buffer overflow vulnerabilities.

findings.title: Hotspot login uses MD5 password hashing
findings.description: The default hotspot captive portal uses MD5 for password hashing (md5.js). MD5 is cryptographically broken and should not be used for password protection.
